fileChanges.diff:
diff --git a/CLAUDE.md b/CLAUDE.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1ec5eb7
--- /dev/null
+++ b/CLAUDE.md
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+# Project Guidelines
+
+## Architecture
+- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
+- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
+- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
+- All business logic lives in the service layer
+
+## Coding Conventions
+- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
+- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
+- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
+- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
+- Prefer async/await over raw Promises
+
+## Testing
+- Tests use vitest
+- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
+- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
+- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method
+
+## File Organization
+- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
+- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
+- src/services/ — Business logic layer
+- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
+- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
+- src/config/ — Configuration files
+- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure
+
+## Git Practices
+- Commit atomically per logical change
+- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
+- Run tests before committing

diff --git a/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts b/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..0a1040d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,26 @@
+/**
+ * NotificationAdapter interface — the contract all delivery channels must implement.
+ *
+ * Architecture constraint: Every notification channel (email, SMS, push, webhook, etc.)
+ * must implement this interface. The NotificationService routes notifications to
+ * adapters based on their supported channel.
+ *
+ * To add a new notification channel:
+ * 1. Add the channel name to NotificationChannel type in src/models/notification.ts
+ * 2. Create a new adapter class implementing NotificationAdapter
+ * 3. Register the adapter with NotificationService via registerAdapter()
+ */
+
+import type { NotificationPayload, NotificationResult, NotificationChannel } from '../models/notification.js';
+
+export interface NotificationAdapter {
+  /** The channel this adapter handles. */
+  readonly channel: NotificationChannel;
+
+  /**
+   * Send a notification through this adapter's channel.
+   * @param payload - The notification to deliver.
+   * @returns The result of the delivery attempt.
+   */
+  send(payload: NotificationPayload): Promise<NotificationResult>;
+}

prompt:
You are Agent 1 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-UR7wak.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Design and implement the foundation of a notification system.

Specifically:
1. Choose an architecture for notifications: event-driven (EventBus with subscribers) OR direct service calls. Commit to ONE approach.
2. Define the notification service interface and core types (NotificationType, NotificationPayload, etc.) in src/services/ and src/models/.
3. Implement the base notification service with the chosen architecture.
4. Create an email notification adapter in src/adapters/email.adapter.ts (stub is fine, but interface must be real).
5. Document your architectural decision — the pattern you chose, why, and what constraints it imposes.
6. Make sure the codebase compiles.

Important:
- Pick ONE pattern (event-driven OR direct calls) and commit to it. Do not mix approaches.
- Define clear interfaces that other developers will implement against.
- Document constraints: e.g., "All adapters must implement NotificationAdapter interface" or "All notifications must go through EventBus.emit()".
- 11 more developers will build on your foundation.

parameters.task: Design and implement notification system foundation: architecture choice (event-driven vs direct calls), core types, notification service, email adapter, and documentation for 11 other developers to build on

parameters.command: ls /private/var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-UR7wak/src/adapters/ 2>/dev/null || echo "adapters dir does not exist"

alternatives.option: Direct service calls — NotificationService called directly by other services
alternatives.reason_rejected: Violates CLAUDE.md guideline. Creates tight coupling between business services and notification logic. Adding new notification triggers requires modifying calling services.
Already partially used but creates coupling, lacks event log/replay, and is synchronous-only. Mixed usage with EventBus is the current problem we're fixing.

alternatives.option: Hybrid approach — use both EventBus and CallbackRegistry depending on context
alternatives.reason_rejected: Current state of the codebase; explicitly flagged as inconsistent and needing unification in notification.service.ts comments.

constraints: All notifications must flow through EventBus.emit() — no direct service calls for triggering notifications
All delivery channels must implement the NotificationAdapter interface
NotificationService subscribes to events and delegates to registered adapters
The CallbackRegistry usage for order status changes must be replaced with EventBus
parameters.context: Building notification system foundation for 11 other developers. The codebase has an existing EventBus and a CallbackRegistry. The NotificationService inconsistently uses both. CLAUDE.md states 'Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling.'
parameters.rationale: EventBus is already well-implemented, CLAUDE.md mandates event-driven approach, and it provides: loose coupling (services emit events without knowing about notifications), extensibility (new notification channels just register as adapters), testability (mock adapters, inspect event log), and event replay capability. The adapter pattern lets developers add new channels (SMS, push, webhook) by implementing a single interface.
parameters.summary: Notification system uses event-driven architecture via EventBus with pluggable adapters

parameters.new_string:
/**
 * Order service — business logic for order management.
 *
 * Depends on:
 * - OrderRepository (data layer) for persistence
 * - UserRepository (data layer) for user validation
 * - EventBus (event system) for publishing order lifecycle events
 *
 * All order events (creation, status changes) are emitted via EventBus.
 * The NotificationService and other listeners subscribe independently.
 */
parameters.old_string:
/**
 * Order service — business logic for order management.
 *
 * Depends on:
 * - OrderRepository (data layer) for persistence
 * - UserRepository (data layer) for user validation
 * - EventBus (event system) for publishing order lifecycle events
 *
 * Architecture note: Order events are used to trigger side effects
 * (notifications, analytics, etc.) without direct coupling.
 * The OrderService emits events; it does not directly call
 * the NotificationService.
 */

parameters.detail:
Completed work:
1. ARCHITECTURE: Chose event-driven pattern via EventBus (per CLAUDE.md guideline). Unified the codebase by removing CallbackRegistry usage from OrderService and NotificationService.
2. CORE TYPES: Created src/models/notification.ts with NotificationType, NotificationChannel, NotificationPayload, NotificationResult, and Notification interfaces.
3. ADAPTER INTERFACE: Created src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts defining the NotificationAdapter contract all delivery channels must implement.
4. EMAIL ADAPTER: Created src/adapters/email.adapter.ts - stub implementation with in-memory storage, real interface.
5. NOTIFICATION SERVICE: Rewrote src/services/notification.service.ts to subscribe to all domain events via EventBus and delegate delivery to registered adapters.
6. ORDER SERVICE: Fixed src/services/order.service.ts to emit status changes via EventBus instead of CallbackRegistry.
7. EXPORTS: Updated src/index.ts with all new types and adapters.
8. TESTS: Updated all tests to use EventBus consistently. All 70 tests pass.

CONSTRAINTS for other developers:
- All notifications MUST flow through EventBus.emit() — no direct service calls
- All delivery channels MUST implement NotificationAdapter interface
- Register adapters via NotificationService.registerAdapter()
- Add new notification types to NotificationType in src/models/notification.ts
- Add new channels to NotificationChannel in src/models/notification.ts
parameters.summary: Notification system foundation implemented: event-driven via EventBus with pluggable adapters

parameters.command:
git add src/models/notification.ts src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts src/adapters/email.adapter.ts src/services/notification.service.ts src/services/order.service.ts src/index.ts tests/services/notification.service.test.ts tests/services/order.service.test.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Implement event-driven notification system with pluggable adapters

Unify the codebase on EventBus for all notification triggers, removing
the inconsistent CallbackRegistry usage. Introduce a NotificationAdapter
interface so delivery channels (email, SMS, push, etc.) can be added by
implementing a single contract and registering with NotificationService.

Key changes:
- Add core notification types (NotificationType, NotificationPayload, etc.)
- Add NotificationAdapter interface for pluggable delivery channels
- Add EmailAdapter stub implementing NotificationAdapter
- Rewrite NotificationService to use only EventBus + adapter delegation
- Fix OrderService to emit status changes via EventBus (was CallbackRegistry)
- Update all tests to use EventBus consistently (70/70 pass)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
